item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like this one, but I agree with Kid F's comment in regards to not being able to really connect with the character of Christian. I like the idea a lot though. Great job.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It is simple, well-paced, and went as expected. No significant flaws, not too flashy, and that is to my taste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Those are fair criticisms and I appreciate them. The main reason the view isn't more nuanced is it's from the perspective of the kid, who doesn't really care about the economy and just wants to see his family together again. In his mind "Reunification=Family together again" and that's all that matters.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm not sure how I feel about this. It's well-written and has good pacing, but ultimately it feels kind of one-note. In attempting to capture some of the emotions associated with the forced separation of an entire nation and eventual reunification, some of the nuance of the relationship between the east and west of the country has been lost. For one, the sad truth is that reunification hasn't been that happy of an ending, particularly for the former East Germany. That part of the country has been, to a large degree, economically and socially crippled after reunification; Mecklenburg-Vorpommern, for instance, has been its population fall significantly since 1990, reflecting a veritable brain drain as people found better job opportunities in the west. It's depressing. It was less of a reunification and more of an annexation, both from a political and economic perspective.

Not just that, but I found it difficult to connect with the kid on an emotional level. He's just not given enough depth to establish a strong pathos, given the emotional weight that this article rests on. However, I do understand and appreciate what you're trying to go for.

I'll have to think about it some more.

item.title: Author's Note
item.content:encoded:
Triumph over bygone sorrow,
Can in unity be won.
For we shall attain a morrow,
When over our Germany,
There is shining sun,
There is shining sun.
- Auferstanden aus Ruinen (Risen From Ruins)

This is a story about a kid that wants his family to be together again.

A lot of people have written Nazi SCPs, and that's all well and good. But there are a lot of other areas of German history that are also interesting - one of which is the time the country was split in two by the Inner German border. I wanted to write a story about German reunification, and figured Little Berlin would be a novel location. Hope you enjoy it!

+ Image Sources
- Mödlareuth picture is public domain via author dedication, picture of the kid is public domain via copyright expiry in both the USA (Pre-1923) and Life+70 countries (author died in 1943).

http://www.gutenberg.org/files/28024/28024-h/28024-h.html#fig75
https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:M%C3%B6dlareuth_(Germany)_before_1989_-_information_table.jpg
